description: Evaluates code for security best practices and compliance requirements. Aligned with AWS AI-DLC Security Baseline (SECURITY-01 through SECURITY-08).

criteria.name: authentication_authorization
criteria.description: Access control is properly implemented and enforced.

scoring.5: All endpoints enforce authentication. Authorization checks verify resource ownership. No privilege escalation paths.
scoring.4: Auth properly implemented with minor gaps in edge case authorization.
scoring.3: Basic auth present but some endpoints or operations lack proper authorization checks.
scoring.2: Auth inconsistently applied. Some endpoints accessible without proper credentials.
scoring.1: No authentication or authorization. Resources accessible by anyone.

criteria.name: injection_prevention
criteria.description: Code is protected against injection attacks (SQL, NoSQL, command, XSS). All API parameters validated for type, length, and format.

scoring.5: All queries use parameterized statements. All user input validated (type, length, format) and sanitized for output context. Request body size limits configured.
scoring.4: Parameterized queries used consistently. Input validation present with minor gaps in format checking.
scoring.3: Most queries parameterized but some string interpolation present. Inconsistent input validation.
scoring.2: Inconsistent use of parameterized queries. Missing input validation on some endpoints.
scoring.1: Raw string concatenation in queries or commands. No input validation.

criteria.name: secret_management
criteria.description: Secrets are stored securely and never exposed in code, logs, or errors.

scoring.5: No secrets in code. Credentials from Secrets Manager/SSM. Secrets excluded from logs and error responses. No PII in log output.
scoring.4: Secrets properly managed with minor logging gaps.
scoring.3: Secrets not in code but may appear in verbose logs or error messages.
scoring.2: Some secrets hard-coded or in configuration files.
scoring.1: Secrets visible in source code, logs, or error responses.

criteria.name: data_protection
criteria.description: Sensitive data is encrypted in transit (TLS 1.2+) and at rest (KMS). PII is handled per policy.

scoring.5: All data encrypted in transit (TLS 1.2+). Sensitive data encrypted at rest with KMS. PII identified and handled per policy. No unnecessary data retention. Object storage rejects non-TLS requests.
scoring.4: Encryption properly configured. Minor gaps in PII handling or data retention.
scoring.3: Basic encryption present. PII handling incomplete. Some stores missing encryption config.
scoring.2: Encryption inconsistent. Some sensitive data unprotected.
scoring.1: No encryption. Sensitive data stored or transmitted in plaintext.

criteria.name: least_privilege
criteria.description: IAM roles and permissions follow least-privilege principle with specific resource ARNs and actions.

scoring.5: All IAM policies scoped to specific resources and actions. No wildcard permissions. Roles are service-specific. Read and write permissions separated.
scoring.4: Mostly least-privilege. Minor over-permission in non-critical areas.
scoring.3: Some wildcard actions or broad resource scopes present. No documented exceptions.
scoring.2: Over-permissive policies. Wildcard actions on broad resource sets.

criteria.name: error_information_leakage
criteria.description: Error responses do not leak internal implementation details, stack traces, or technology identifiers.

scoring.5: All errors return generic messages to clients. Stack traces, file paths, version numbers, and internal details logged server-side only. No technology identifiers in responses.
scoring.4: Errors generally safe. Minor information leakage in edge cases.
scoring.3: Some error responses include internal details like file paths or library names.
scoring.2: Stack traces or database errors exposed to clients in some cases.
scoring.1: Detailed stack traces, SQL errors, or internal paths exposed to clients.

criteria.name: http_security_headers
criteria.description: HTTP security headers are set on all HTML-serving endpoints (CSP, HSTS, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy).

scoring.5: All required headers set: CSP (no unsafe-inline/eval), HSTS (max-age >= 31536000), X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, X-Frame-Options: DENY, Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin.
scoring.4: Most headers set. Minor gaps in CSP strictness.
scoring.3: Some security headers present. CSP or HSTS missing.

criteria.name: structured_logging
criteria.description: Application uses structured logging with correlation IDs, directed to a centralized log service.

scoring.5: Structured logging framework configured. Every log entry has timestamp, correlation ID, log level. Output directed to CloudWatch/centralized service. Access logging enabled on all network intermediaries.
scoring.4: Structured logging present. Minor gaps in correlation ID propagation.
scoring.3: Logging present but not structured. Missing correlation IDs or centralized routing.
scoring.2: Ad-hoc console.log/print used as primary logging. No centralized routing.
scoring.1: No logging infrastructure. Ad-hoc print statements only.

criteria.name: dependency_security
criteria.description: No known vulnerable dependencies. Versions pinned. Unused dependencies removed.

scoring.5: No known vulnerabilities in dependencies. All versions pinned in lockfile. Unused dependencies removed. Transitive deps reviewed.
scoring.4: No critical vulnerabilities. Versions mostly pinned. Minor unused deps remain.
scoring.3: Some moderate vulnerabilities present. Lockfile exists but not all versions pinned.
scoring.2: Known vulnerabilities in dependencies. No lockfile or unpinned versions.
scoring.1: Critical vulnerabilities in dependencies. No dependency management.

criteria.name: security_agent_findings
criteria.description: No unresolved Critical/High findings from AWS Security Agent on this codebase.

scoring.5: No open findings from Security Agent. All historical findings remediated within SLA.
scoring.4: No Critical/High findings. Minor (LOW/MEDIUM) findings with active remediation plans.
scoring.3: Open HIGH findings with remediation in progress. No Critical.
scoring.2: Open CRITICAL findings or HIGH findings past remediation SLA.
scoring.1: Multiple CRITICAL findings or validated pen test exploits unresolved.

output_format.overall_score: float 0-1 (weighted average of criteria scores normalized to 0-1)
output_format.criteria_scores: object mapping criteria name to { score: 1-5, reasoning: string }
output_format.security_findings: array of { rule_id: string, severity: string, description: string }
